summary:
The absence of direct statements linking tumor-associated macrophages (TAMs) to prognosis is offset by strong circumstantial evidence through their co-localization with recurrent lesions in the peritumoral brain zone—an area associated with adverse outcomes. This spatial relationship implies a probable negative prognostic effect, aligning with existing neuro-oncological understanding of macrophage roles in glioma aggressiveness.  

Final boxed response: \boxed{A} 

(Note: The summary focuses only on essential clinical rationale; no extraneous details included.)  

--- 
End of
